title: Sprint 23 Retrospective - Missed Deliverables Review
transcript_text:
[Sarah Chen] (00:00:10): Alright team, we need to have a frank discussion. Sprint 23 did not go well. Three of our five major deliverables were not completed. Let's go through them.
[Sarah Chen] (00:00:30): John, the payment gateway integration was supposed to be done last Friday. What happened?
[John Park] (00:00:45): I got pulled into the production outage on Wednesday and lost two full days. The payment integration is 80% done but the error handling and retry logic still needs work. I estimate 3 more days.
[Sarah Chen] (00:01:05): That outage wasn't even in your area. Why were you the one fixing it?
[John Park] (00:01:18): Nobody else was available. David was out sick and Tom was on PTO. I was the only one who knew the database layer well enough.
[Sarah Chen] (00:01:35): We need better on-call rotation. That's an action item - someone needs to set up a proper on-call schedule. David, the user authentication redesign?
[David Kim] (00:01:52): I completed the backend changes but the security review got delayed. The external auditor canceled twice. It's now scheduled for next Tuesday.
[Sarah Chen] (00:02:10): That's not entirely your fault, but we should have had a backup plan. Can you follow up with the auditor and confirm Tuesday is firm? If they cancel again, we go with our internal security review.
[David Kim] (00:02:28): Got it. I'll send them a formal confirmation request today.
[Sarah Chen] (00:02:38): Maria, the customer onboarding flow redesign - I see it's only 40% complete.
[Maria Lopez] (00:02:52): Honestly, I was spread too thin. I had the onboarding flow, two bug fixes that got escalated as P0s, and I was covering for a teammate who quit. The scope of the onboarding project was also larger than estimated.
[Sarah Chen] (00:03:15): That's a workload issue and an estimation issue. We need to get better at both. Alex, at least tell me the test automation framework is done?
[Alex Rivera] (00:03:30): The framework is set up and running. But we only have coverage for 30% of the critical paths. The original target was 70%. I need at least another sprint to hit that.
[Sarah Chen] (00:03:50): Okay. So here's what we're going to do. Priority one: John finishes the payment integration by Wednesday. No interruptions - I'll handle any production issues myself if needed.
[Sarah Chen] (00:04:05): Priority two: David gets the security review confirmed and completed by end of next week. Priority three: Maria - I'm pulling you off bug duty. Focus solely on the onboarding flow for the next two weeks.
[Maria Lopez] (00:04:25): Thank you. That will make a huge difference.
[Sarah Chen] (00:04:35): Alex, continue the test automation but revise the target to 50% coverage this sprint, 70% next sprint. And everyone - if you're blocked or overloaded, speak up immediately. Don't wait until the retrospective to tell me things went wrong.
[Alex Rivera] (00:04:55): Understood. I'll also document the current coverage gaps so we can prioritize which paths to automate next.
[Sarah Chen] (00:05:10): Good. Last thing - we need to establish a proper handoff process for when people are unavailable. John shouldn't have been the only person who could fix that outage. Knowledge sharing sessions every other Friday starting next week. I'll set them up.
